item.title: Blog
item.description: Sharpen your defenses with the latest threat insights, industry trends and product updates sure to put you one step ahead of hackers – and your competition.

item.title: From Chrome to Everywhere: The Ever-Expanding CVE-2023-4863
item.description: CVE-2023-4863 impacts many web browsers, operating systems, and software. These far-reaching impacts emphasize the urgency of patching.

item.title: FBI and CISA Team Up to Combat Snatch Ransomware
item.description: Read the APG's thoughts on the FBI and CISA's advisory on Snatch Ransomware. With shared community intel, we're able to defeat cybercrime.

item.title: Patch It Up: WS_FTP Server's Unplanned Fixes
item.description: A vulnerability in Progress Software's WS_FTP Server software allows threat actors to execute remote commands & perform unauthorized file operations.

item.title: APG Threat Digest: 12th Edition
item.description: Read the APG's weekly recap on Progress Software and Confluence vulnerabilities, "Menorah" malware, an unprotected RSS database, and more.

item.title: Blackpoint’s New Chapter with Google Workspace
item.description: Blackpoint Cyber has released its cloud security offering for Google Workspace. It is available through Cloud Response now.

item.title: FIN8 Strikes Again: Advanced Tactics in Recent Cyber Assault
item.description: A recent cyberattack by FIN8 showcased their use of compromised RDP, network scanning, RMM disabling, & the establishment of C2 channels.

item.title: 5 Steps to Securing the Cloud
item.description: Are you wondering how to best secure your cloud environment? Read Blackpoint's blog for five steps towards safety take immediately.

item.title: Understanding Business Email Compromise: A Threat to Modern Enterprises
item.description: Business Email Compromise attacks can cause financial and informational loss. In our blog, learn what it entails & how to defend against it.

item.title: Unmasking Ransomed.vc: The New Kid on the Cyber Block
item.description: Ransomwed.vc has been active since August, targeting Sony and working with groups like Everest Ransomware. Learn more in the APG's blog.

item.title: Qakbot’s Quack Attack Comes to an End
item.description: The notorious Qakbot botnet, which poisoned organizations with malware and ransomware globally, has been dismantled. Here's the story and why it matters.

item.title: Proxy Party of Peril: Malware and Proxy Team Up
item.description: A new threat targeting Windows systems allows hackers to use a proxy server application to install malware, turning devices into a botnet.

item.title: China’s High-Stakes Cyber Shenanigans: Cert Theft & DLL Hijacking
item.description: China-based BRONZE STARLIGHT group is conducting cyberattacks against the Southeast Asian gambling sector.

item.title: MalDoc in PDF: The Word Doc and PDF Team Up
item.description: An attack method dubbed “MalDoc in PDF” involves a polyglot technique of embedding a Word document with malicious macros inside a PDF.

item.title: Cisco ASA SSL VPN Appliances Under Fire
item.description: Attackers have been targeting Cisco ASA SSL VPN devices by exploiting weak passwords and the lack of multi-factor authentication.

item.title: Ways to Win in December
item.description: Join the Blackpoint team this month as we celebrate the holidays with SOC'ing Stuffers and Channel Program's Holidaze.

item.title: APG Threat Digest: 13th Edition
item.description: In our latest Threat Digest we discuss FIN8, DarkGate, HelloKitty, curl vulnerabilities, Wordpress malware, and US government advice.

item.title: APG Threat Digest: 14th Edition
item.description: Learn how threat actors are exploiting QR codes, a Confluence vulnerability, malware, LinkedIn, and cloud computing.

item.title: Contextualizing Identity-Based Attacks for a More Secure Cloud
item.description: Learn how Identity Response for Azure AD in Blackpoint's Cloud Response enhances security against cloud-based attacks by contextualizing Azure SSO logins.

item.title: Scan with Caution: The Light and Dark Sides of QR Codes
item.description: Learn all about QR codes and why the square barcodes can pose a security threat with the latest APG threat intel!

item.title: Shedding Light on Cyberthreats: APT34's Menorah Malware
item.description: The APG discusses the discovery of the Menorah malware, attributed to APT34, and highlights the importance of monitoring scheduled tasks.

item.title: When Vulnerabilities Collide: Juniper’s Critical Collision
item.description: Vulnerabilities have been found in Juniper Networks' EX switches and SRX firewalls that could lead to remote code execution if exploited together.

item.title: Top Five Cloud Security Threats
item.description: Check out Blackpoint's top five cloud security threats according to our 24/7 Security Operations Center, powered by Cloud Response.

item.title: APG Threat Digest: 15th Edition
item.description: In this week’s Threat Digest: Octo Tempest, a patch for Citrix Bleed, Hunters International ransomware, Microsoft Exchange targeted, and VMware's critical fix.

item.title: The Fatal Four: Overcoming Common MSP Pain Points
item.description: Learn how to address talent shortages, lackluster GTM strategies, vendor sprawl, and customer loss with Blackpoint Cyber.

item.title: APG Threat Digest: 16th Edition
item.description: APG Threat Digest: 16th Edition

item.title: Top Ten Cybersecurity Best Practices
item.description: Prepare for a more secure future with Blackpoint's top ten cybersecurity best practices. To learn about each one, read the blog.

item.title: Join Blackpoint Cyber's Aussie Adventure!
item.description: Blackpoint Cyber will be in Australia for DattoCon APAC and more! We'd love to meet with Managed Service Providers in Sydney and Melbourne.

item.title: What is True MDR? (Or, How to Spot a Fake)
item.description: How do you know if your MDR service (or the one you’re considering) is true MDR or just an imitation? We break down what MDR means.

item.title: MSPs’ Role in Shining a Light on the Dark Web
item.description: MSPs’ Role in Shining a Light on the Dark Web
